question.text: In retirement income planning, the sequence of returns risk refers to:

answer.text: The order in which different investments mature
The risk that poor investment returns in the early years of retirement disproportionately deplete the corpus
The sequence in which the subscriber selects pension funds
The order of priority for paying expenses

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Sequence of returns risk means that negative returns in the early years of retirement, when withdrawals are being made, can permanently reduce the corpus — even if average returns over the full period are adequate. **The Trap:** This is different from average return risk. Two retirees with the same average return can have very different outcomes depending on WHEN the bad years occur. **Cross-Exam Context:** Sequence of returns risk is an advanced retirement planning concept in Series XVII. It connects to the rationale behind the bucket strategy and SWP timing. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The bucket strategy specifically mitigates sequence of returns risk by ensuring that short-term expenses are funded from stable assets, allowing growth assets time to recover from downturns.
